behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is between JSON parsing and downstream hook-input handling (multiple modules). A unit test of parser-only logic would miss wiring regressions where parsed markdown-heavy fields are mishandled after parse.
behaviors.test_description: Run hook input handling in-process with real parser + real input-handler wiring and an in-memory trace sink. Feed valid JSON containing code fences, backticks, and `$vars`; assert parse succeeds, no parse-failure trace is emitted, and handler reaches normal success path.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on interaction between parser error output and trace-record construction. Parser-only unit tests do not verify that diagnostic context is propagated into telemetry correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Execute parser+trace-construction path with malformed JSON fixtures (without synthetic trailing-junk shortcuts only). Assert `json_parse_failed` is emitted and includes expected context fields (error location/snippet/correlation metadata) sufficient to identify malformed region.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is policy mapping across modules (parse error -> policy decision -> telemetry intent marker). A unit test of policy logic alone can miss contract mismatches in integration.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the local hook-input orchestration with malformed JSON and verify: no exception escapes, returned result is handled/failure-coded as designed, and telemetry-intent flag/state is set for downstream emission.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly depends on subprocess + stdin/stdout/stderr/exit behavior. Mocking process I/O can hide real crash/flush/exit-order failures.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the real hook executable/script as a subprocess, pipe malformed stdin, wait for exit, and assert: process does not hard-crash, exits via expected handled path, and trace signal is observable before/at exit.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: The behavior validates end-to-end invocation wiring including real stdin ingestion and trace artifact emission mechanism; these are OS/process/file or IPC boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end hook invocation using real process entrypoint and configured real trace sink target (e.g., file/artifact channel). Supply malformed stdin and assert the persisted/emitted artifact contains `json_parse_failed` with expected identifiers.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: This is a classification/regression boundary across parser + failure-attribution/trace logic. Unit tests can validate parser cases, but integration is needed to ensure attribution is correct in the actual handling path.
behaviors.test_description: Add paired fixtures through the same in-process handling pipeline: (A) valid JSON with markdown-heavy fields and (B) malformed JSON. Assert only (B) emits `json_parse_failed`, while (A) reaches normal path with no parse-failure attribution.
